text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a lush forest, there lived a clever squirrel named Olive. Olive had a knack for finding the newest berries and could tell weather forecasts without listening to the birds. One sunny afternoon, while munching on an exquisite blackberry pie she'd crafted herself, Olive listened attentively as a gentle rain started to fall.

The rain wasn*spilled* marvelous sequoia berries down the tree branch. Inspired, Olive decided to weave them into a tapestry depicting her favorite stories—a retelling of her favorite legends about forest deities. She sewed all night, dreaming vividly of battles fought against dark clouds, miraculous rescues by woodland spirits, and sunrises heralding seasons change.

The next morning, she unfurled her creation. The tapestry was beautiful, each berry telling a story. As Olive sat admiring her work, a wise owl perched nearby. "You've woven something of great beauty," the owl whispered. "But remember
